name: eval-2-knowing-when-to-stop
query: Build me a React dashboard for my sales team. Interrogate me.
context_setup: User answers: 8 account executives, daily use to prioritize outreach, must integrate with Salesforce, dark mode preferred, no specific deadline, lives at /sales-dash route. After 3-4 questions Claude has: clear goal (prioritize outreach), audience (8 AEs, daily), constraints (Salesforce integration, dark mode), format (React dashboard), and at least one surfaced assumption (probably: 'I'm assuming you want this responsive for mobile too — confirm or correct').
expected_behavior: Asks 3-5 focused questions, one per turn, each building on previous answers
Stops asking once it can confidently answer all four: what the output is, who it's for, the constraints that would make it fail, and at least one surfaced assumption the user didn't state
Does NOT keep asking once those four are covered
For complex tasks (this is one): presents a structured brief before executing — Goal / Audience / Constraints / Format / Assumptions
Waits for confirmation before starting the actual build
If user says 'go ahead' or 'that's everything' at any point, stops immediately and proceeds even if more questions remained
failure_modes_to_check: Asks 8+ questions because there are still edges to explore (over-interrogation)
Skips the structured brief and dives into building
Does not surface any assumptions in the brief (Layer 4 gap)
